data.title: Microservices-based Architecture
data.description: Amazon utilizes a microservices architecture which allows developers to deploy services independently.
data.technical_spec: Service-oriented architecture, with APIs facilitating communication between distributed services.

data.title: Scalable Infrastructure
data.description: Amazon's infrastructure is designed to scale horizontally to accommodate varying workloads.
data.technical_spec: Auto-scaling groups, load balancers, and distributed databases.

data.title: Cloud Deployment
data.description: Leveraging AWS cloud infrastructure to ensure availability and reliability.
data.technical_spec: Elastic Compute Cloud (EC2), Simple Storage Service (S3), and Route 53 for DNS.

data.title: Content Delivery Network
data.description: Amazon uses its CDN, CloudFront, to deliver content efficiently.
data.technical_spec: Edge locations distributed globally for faster content delivery.

data.title: Database Management
data.description: Usage of NoSQL and SQL databases to handle different types of data workloads.
data.technical_spec: Amazon Aurora, DynamoDB for NoSQL data stores.
